channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan rejects India's unilateral suspension of Indus Waters Treaty at UN World Water Day event
item.description: During a high-level United Nations event marking World Water Day with focus on water and gender equality, Pakistan exercised its right of reply to counter remarks made by the Indian representative. Pa...

item.title: World Cup travel could be affected by rising airfares and flight disruptions
item.description: Soccer fans planning to attend this summer’s World Cup are being cautioned about potential increases in airfares and disruptions to flights. Aviation expert Anita Mendiratta explains that the ongoing ...

item.title: High Court rules in favour of cremation for 17-year-old who died by suicide after parents' dispute over burial
item.description: A High Court judge has ruled that the body of 17-year-old Gabrielle Barbus, who tragically took his own life on 30 December 2025 after struggling with mental health issues, should be cremated and his ...

item.title: DSWD Holds Special Payout of Financial Aid for Tricycle Drivers in the Philippines
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) organized a special relief payout day to provide much-needed financial assistance to tricycle drivers across the country, particularly in the Na...

item.title: Governor’s Brother Accused of Assaulting Delta Kickboxing Coach Over Unpaid ₦200,000 Debt
item.description: Governor Sheriff Oborevwori’s younger brother, Onoriode Oborevwori, has been accused of assaulting Steve Ogom Momah, the Delta State Head Coach of Kickboxing, over an unpaid debt of more than ₦200,000...

item.title: Mirwaiz Umar Farooq Alleges Arbitrary House Arrest for Third Friday in Ramzan, Barred from Leading Prayers at Jamia Masjid
item.description: Kashmir's chief cleric and Hurriyat Conference leader Mirwaiz Umar Farooq has alleged that he was placed under arbitrary house arrest at his residence in Nigeen, Srinagar, for the third consecutive Fr...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif Holds Phone Calls with Azerbaijan and Malaysia Leaders, Urges De-escalation in Middle East Conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Friday engaged in telephone conversations with Azerbaijan's President Ilham Aliyev and Malaysia's Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim amid the ongoing hostilities in the Midd...

item.title: PMD forecasts rain and thunderstorms in Karachi and Sindh districts on Eidul Fitr first day
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a forecast indicating that rain with thunderstorms and strong winds is likely in Karachi Division and several districts of Sindh province on Sat...

item.title: American tourist detained in Bali for walking during Nyepi Day of Silence
item.description: A 57-year-old American tourist was detained by local security in Bali's Sukawati village on Thursday morning around 7am during Nyepi, the island's annual Day of Silence. Nyepi is a sacred Balinese Hin...

item.title: The case for consolidating South Africa's municipalities into fewer, more viable entities
item.description: South Africa's local government system is gradually deteriorating, with many of the 257 municipalities struggling to deliver basic services like clean water, reliable electricity, and maintained roads...

item.title: Congregants Fight to Collect 'Anointed' Soil After Pastor Ezekiel Odero Walks Over It
item.description: Pastor Ezekiel Odero, a well-known preacher from Kilifi, has stirred controversy once again after a video surfaced showing his followers scrambling to collect soil that he stepped on. This event took ...

item.title: Tanzania Reports Progress to UN on Reducing Gender-Based Violence and FGM Prevalence
item.description: Tanzania has stepped up its fight against gender-based violence and female genital mutilation (FGM), showing good results through strong national plans. Minister for Community Development, Gender, Wom...

item.title: Italian family ordered to pay £150,000 costs after losing parking rights dispute linked to unlawful car repairs in north London lane
item.description: An Italian family living in Arnos Grove, north London, has been ordered to pay around £150,000 in legal costs after unsuccessfully applying to the Land Registry for a historical right to park in a sha...

item.title: Nigerian leaders felicitate Muslims on Eid-el-Fitr, call for peace, unity and national reflection
item.description: My people, Muslim faithful all over the world dey celebrate Eid-el-Fitr today to mark the end of the holy month of Ramadan wey dem fast, pray and do charity. For Naija, plenty big men come out with me...

item.title: French Nuclear Doctrine Shift to 'Advanced Deterrence' in Europe Questioned Through Historical Opacity in Algeria
item.description: On 2 March 2026, President Emmanuel Macron announced major changes to France's nuclear policy. France will increase its nuclear warheads for the first time since 1992 and introduce 'dissuasion avancée...

item.title: Labour Party, Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan urge Nigerians to reflect and promote unity this Eid-el-Fitr
item.description: As Muslims don dey celebrate Eid-el-Fitr, wey mark the end of Ramadan fasting, Labour Party and Kogi Central lawmaker, Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan, don call on Nigerians to dey reflect soberly and renew t...

item.title: OpenAI's post-Tumbler Ridge commitments represent user surveillance rather than genuine AI system regulation
item.description: Following the tragic mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, on February 10, 2026, where 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar killed eight people and injured many others before taking her own li...

item.title: National Artist Ricky Lee Calls for Proper Recognition and Fair Compensation for Filipino Scriptwriters
item.description: Renowned National Artist for Film and Broadcast Arts, Ricky Lee, has voiced out his strong call for better treatment of scriptwriters in the Philippine entertainment industry. In a recent statement, t...

item.title: Gauteng Schools Face Electricity Cuts Amid Unpaid Municipal Bills
item.description: In Gauteng this week, at least 36 schools were disconnected from electricity due to unpaid municipal bills, with over a hundred more reportedly at risk. These are predominantly no-fee schools, already...

item.title: Latest Political Cartoons by David Rowe for March 2026
item.description: The article presents a collection of political cartoons by renowned Australian cartoonist David Rowe for March 2026. These cartoons continue Rowe's tradition of visually commenting on federal politics...
